ops.title: gitignore sweep folds capt-hook.toml consumer cleanup (claimed by session e9a3a2be)
ops.body:
Commit 0fd6aae9 (capt-hook v11 zero-config, session d97c4285) removed `plugin/guides/toml/capt-hook-*` from the pack. Consequence: every consumer repo still carrying a committed `.claude/fragments/.claude/capt-hook.toml/` layout fails ANY full `cc-guides render` with `unknown fragment: cc-skills:capt-hook-base (kind toml)` — including the daily guides.yml re-render crons, fleet-wide, from today.

The in-flight root-.gitignore migration sweep (session e9a3a2be, cc-guides 0.1.42 gitignore kind + cc-skills gitignore fragments, commit 65ccbdd0) is performing the documented consumer cleanup in the same per-repo commit its lanes already make: delete `.claude/capt-hook.toml` + its fragments layout (per hooks.md v11: "delete it (and its fragments layout) from older scaffolds"), add the `.claude/fragments/.gitignore/` layout, full render at @main, ship.

If you are session d97c4285 planning your own consumer-side sweep: don't — the gitignore sweep covers all repos carrying `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock` (44 as of 2026-07-20). Repos already cleaned by you are naturally skipped (no capt-hook.toml layout → nothing to delete).
